back_translation: Become followers-of-the-pattern of me, just as I myself follow-the-pattern of Christ.
Now I commend you all that you remember me in everything, and that you hold to the handed-down teachings according to what I have given to you.
But I want you all to understand that the head of every man is Christ; the head of the woman is the man; and the head of Christ is God.
Every man who prays or prophesies while having something on his head dishonors his own head.
And every woman who prays or prophesies with her head not covered dishonors her own head; because it is just like having one's head shaved already.
Because if a woman does not cover her head, then let her hair be cut off; but if cutting hair or shaving hair is a shameful thing for a woman, then let her cover her head.
Because a man should not cover his head, since he is the (royal) image and the (royal) glory of God; while the woman is the glory of the man.
Because the man did not come from the woman; rather, the woman came from the man.
And the man was not created for the woman; rather, the woman was created for the man.
For this reason, the woman ought to have a sign of authority on her head, because of the angels.
However, in the Lord, neither is the woman set apart from the man, nor is the man set apart from the woman.
Because just as the woman comes from the man, in the same way also the man is born by way of the woman; but all things come from God.
Consider this in your own minds: is it appropriate that a woman should pray to God with her head not covered?
Does nature itself not teach you all that, if a man wears long hair, it is a dishonor to him?
But if a woman wears long hair, it is honor for her? Because long hair has been (royally) given to her as a covering.
But if anyone is inclined to argue — we have no such custom, and the churches of God have no such custom either.
In the following instruction, I cannot commend you all, because the fact that you all gather together does not lead to a better result, but leads to a worse result.
Firstly, I have heard that when you all gather together as a church, there are divisions among you all; and I believe in part.
Because there must be also faction-splittings among you all, in order that those who are approved among you all may be made evident.
Therefore, when you all gather together at the same place, it is not the eating of the meal of the Lord.
Because at the time of eating, each person hurries to eat his own meal first; some are hungry, while some get drunk.

Do you all not have your own homes in which to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God, and shame those who do not have anything? What shall I say to you all? Shall I commend you all in this matter? In this matter, I am not able to commend you.
Because what I have received from the (royal) Lord, I have also handed on to you all — that is, the Lord Jesus, on the night when He was being (royally) handed-over-by-betrayal, took up bread.
And when He had (royally) given thanks, He (royally) broke it apart and (royally) said, "This is my body, which is given for you all; do this in order to remember me."
In the same way, when they had eaten the meal, He took up the cup, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood; do this every time that you all drink, in order to remember me."
Because every time that you all eat this bread and drink this cup, you all proclaim the (royal) death of the Lord, until He (royally) comes.
Therefore, anyone who eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an inappropriate manner will be one who is guilty against the (royal) body and (royal) blood of the Lord.
Therefore, let a person examine himself well; then go ahead and eat from the bread and drink from the cup.
Because the one who eats and drinks without perceiving the (royal) body, eats and drinks judgment upon himself.
For this reason, among you all there are many who are weak and sick, and a considerable number have already passed away.
But if we examine ourselves correctly, we would not be subjected to judgment.
But when we are judged by the (royal) Lord, we receive disciplinary instruction, in order that we will not be condemned together with the world.
Therefore, my brothers and sisters, when you all come together to eat the meal, wait for one another.
If anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, so that your gathering will not lead to judgment. As for the other matters that remain, I myself will arrange them when I come.
